description: Social calendar app that helps friends and family stay connected by surfacing mutual availability and suggesting meetups. White/gold/dark-green/black design, macOS font, mobile-first responsive UI.

userStories.title: Design System & MUI Theme Setup
userStories.description: Create a custom MUI theme with Circl design tokens: white/gold (#C6993A)/dark-green (#1B5E20)/black palette, SF Pro / -apple-system font stack, consistent spacing (4px base), border radius (8/12/16px), and subtle shadows. Add dark mode support. All existing components must inherit the theme.
acceptanceCriteria: MUI theme created with all design tokens from PRD section 6.1
Font family set to -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, SF Pro Display, SF Pro Text, Helvetica Neue, sans-serif
Color palette: dark green primary, gold accent, white background, black text
Dark mode theme variant defined
Theme applied globally via ThemeProvider in App.js
userStories.notes: Created custom MUI theme with all Circl design tokens from PRD 6.1. Includes dark green primary (#1B5E20), gold accent (#C6993A), SF Pro font stack, 4px spacing base, 8/12/16px border radii, subtle/medium/elevated shadows, and full dark mode variant. Theme applied globally via ThemeProvider wrapping App.js with CssBaseline. Dark mode persists to localStorage. 19 theme tests + 1 existing test all pass.

userStories.title: Auth Pages Redesign (Sign In / Sign Up)
userStories.description: Redesign the sign-in and sign-up pages with modern, clean aesthetic. White background with subtle gold/green accents. Prominent 'Continue with Google' CTA (dark green button with gold icon). Minimal email/password as secondary option. Add trust messaging about privacy. Fully responsive mobile-first layout.
acceptanceCriteria: Clean white background with subtle geometric or line-art accents in gold/green
Continue with Google button is primary CTA (dark green, large, gold icon)
Email/password is secondary (outlined, minimal style)
Privacy trust line displayed: 'We only see when you're free — never your event details'
Responsive: looks great on 320px through 1440px+
Smooth transition between sign-in and sign-up views
Error states styled consistently with design system
userStories.notes: Redesigned Sign In and Sign Up pages with modern, clean aesthetic. Created shared AuthLayout component with Circl branding, subtle geometric gold/green circle and line accents, and privacy trust messaging. Google OAuth "Continue with Google" is the primary CTA (dark green button with gold icon, large size, elevated shadow). Email/password is secondary (outlined, minimal style). Both pages use MUI Fade transitions for smooth appearance. Fully responsive mobile-first layout (works 320px through 1440px+). Error states use MUI Alert with dismiss button, styled consistently with design system. 27 new tests covering rendering, validation, error states, and privacy messaging. All 47 tests pass.

userStories.title: Responsive Layout Shell (Mobile Nav + Desktop Sidebar)
userStories.description: Rebuild the app layout shell. Mobile: bottom tab bar (Calendar, Friends, Suggestions, Profile) + top app bar. Desktop: collapsible left sidebar (not fixed 340px) + top app bar. Remove the current fixed-width Drawer approach. Add proper responsive breakpoints (xs/sm/md/lg/xl).
acceptanceCriteria: Mobile (< 900px): bottom tab navigation with 4 tabs — Calendar, Friends, Suggestions, Profile
Mobile: top app bar with notification bell and hamburger for secondary actions
Desktop (>= 900px): collapsible left sidebar with navigation links and calendar filters
Desktop: top app bar with search, notifications
Old fixed 340px Sidebar component replaced
Smooth transitions on sidebar collapse/expand
All breakpoints render correctly: 320px, 600px, 900px, 1200px, 1536px
userStories.notes: Rebuilt the app layout shell with full responsive support. Mobile (<900px): bottom tab navigation with 4 tabs (Calendar, Friends, Suggestions, Profile) + top app bar with notification bell and hamburger menu for secondary actions (profile, theme toggle, logout). Desktop (>=900px): collapsible left sidebar with navigation links, calendar filter checkboxes, and smooth expand/collapse transitions + top app bar with search bar, notifications, theme toggle, profile, and logout. Removed old fixed 340px Sidebar/Drawer approach. Added proper MUI breakpoints (xs:0, sm:600, md:900, lg:1200, xl:1536). Layout uses React Router Outlet pattern so all authenticated pages render inside AppLayout. 74 tests pass including 22 new layout tests covering all breakpoints.

userStories.title: Calendar Component Replacement
userStories.description: Replace the hand-rolled dayjs calendar grid with FullCalendar (@fullcalendar/react) or Schedule-X. Support Month, Week, and Day views. Mobile: month view shows dots for events (tap to expand), swipe to navigate. Events color-coded: local=dark green, Google=gold, friend availability=charcoal. Integrate with existing Redux event state.
acceptanceCriteria: Calendar library integrated (FullCalendar or Schedule-X)
Month, Week, and Day views available with view toggle
Events render as color-coded chips/dots matching design tokens
Mobile: month view compact with dot indicators, tap day to see events
Mobile: swipe left/right to navigate periods
Existing local events from eventSlice display correctly
Existing Google events from googleEventSlice display correctly
Event click opens event detail modal/bottom sheet
Calendar is fully responsive across all breakpoints
userStories.notes: Core calendar experience. Evaluate FullCalendar vs Schedule-X — pick whichever integrates better with MUI theme.

userStories.title: Event Modals Redesign (Create + Manage)
userStories.description: Redesign EventCreatorModal and EventManagerModal. Mobile: bottom sheet (slides up from bottom). Desktop: centered modal. Replace raw datetime-local inputs with proper MUI date/time pickers. Style with design system tokens. Keep Google Calendar sync checkbox.
acceptanceCriteria: Mobile: modals render as bottom sheets (slide up animation)
Desktop: modals render as centered dialogs
MUI DateTimePicker replaces raw datetime-local inputs
All form fields styled with design tokens (green/gold/white/black)
Google Calendar sync checkbox retained and styled
Create and edit flows both work correctly
Delete confirmation dialog styled consistently
Form validation with clear error states

userStories.title: Profile Page Redesign
userStories.description: Redesign UserProfile page. Add avatar upload/display, display name field, home city field (new). Clean card-based layout. Connected accounts section showing Google Calendar status. Responsive design.
acceptanceCriteria: Avatar display with upload capability (or initials fallback)
Display name, first name, last name, email fields
Home city field (new — needed for location features)
Birthday and timezone fields retained
Connected accounts section showing Google Calendar link status
Save changes with success feedback
Fully responsive card-based layout
Styled with design system tokens
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 theme being in place. New city field requires backend user model update.

userStories.title: Onboarding Flow (Post First Login)
userStories.description: Add a multi-step onboarding flow after first Google OAuth login. Step 1: Set display name + avatar. Step 2: Set home city. Step 3: Invite friends (email input). Step 4: Privacy explainer. Store onboarding_complete flag on user.
acceptanceCriteria: Onboarding triggers on first login (onboarding_complete = false)
4-step wizard with progress indicator
Step 1: display name + avatar upload
Step 2: city input with autocomplete
Step 3: invite friends by email
Step 4: brief privacy explainer with 'Get Started' CTA
onboarding_complete flag saved to user record
Can be skipped but encouraged
Responsive and styled with design tokens
userStories.notes: Requires new DB columns: display_name, city, avatar_url, onboarding_complete on users table.

userStories.title: Friend System Backend (DB + API)
userStories.description: Implement the friendship and circles backend. Create friendships table (requester, addressee, status: pending/accepted/blocked). Create circles and circle_members tables. Build REST API endpoints for friend requests, accept/decline, list friends, CRUD circles, add/remove circle members. Add auth middleware protection.
acceptanceCriteria: friendships table created with proper constraints and indexes
circles and circle_members tables created
POST /friends/request — send friend request by email
PATCH /friends/:id/accept — accept request
PATCH /friends/:id/decline — decline request
DELETE /friends/:id — remove friend
GET /friends — list all accepted friends
GET /friends/requests — list pending incoming requests
POST /circles — create a named circle
POST /circles/:id/members — add friend to circle
DELETE /circles/:id/members/:userId — remove from circle
GET /circles — list user's circles with members
All endpoints protected by ensureLoggedIn middleware
Input validation with JSON schemas
Unit tests for models, integration tests for routes
userStories.notes: Core social feature. No frontend dependency — can be built in parallel with UI work.

userStories.title: Friend System Frontend (UI + Redux)
userStories.description: Build the Friends page and friend management UI. Friends list grouped by circles. Friend cards with avatar, name, city. Add Friend flow (search by email). Pending requests section. Create/manage circles. Redux friendSlice for state management.
acceptanceCriteria: Friends page accessible via bottom tab (mobile) and sidebar (desktop)
Search bar to find and add friends by email
Pending requests section with accept/decline buttons
Friends list grouped by circles
Friend card: avatar, name, city, friendship status
Create circle dialog with name input
Add/remove friends from circles
Redux friendSlice with async thunks for all API calls
Loading and error states handled
Empty states with helpful messaging
Fully responsive, styled with design tokens
userStories.notes: Depends on US-008 backend being ready.

userStories.title: FreeBusy API Integration (Backend)
userStories.description: Build a backend proxy for Google Calendar FreeBusy API. Accepts a list of friend user IDs, fetches their Google Calendar free/busy data (using their stored encrypted access tokens), and returns merged availability windows. Privacy: only returns free/busy blocks, never event details. Friends must have accepted friendship + opted in to sharing.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /freebusy endpoint accepts friend IDs and date range
Fetches FreeBusy data from Google Calendar API for each friend
Uses stored encrypted access tokens (decrypted server-side)
Only returns free/busy time blocks — never event titles or details
Checks friendship status (must be accepted)
Checks sharing opt-in preference
Handles token refresh for expired access tokens
Returns normalized time blocks per friend
Error handling for invalid tokens, API failures
userStories.notes: Depends on US-008 friendships. Requires Google Calendar FreeBusy scope.

userStories.title: Find a Time — Shared Availability View
userStories.description: Build the 'Find a Time' UI. User selects friend(s) or circle, sees a week/day view with overlaid free/busy blocks. Mutual free windows highlighted in green. Tap a free window to create a hangout event pre-filled with time + friend as invitee.
acceptanceCriteria: Friend/circle picker to select who to compare availability with
Week view showing overlaid free/busy blocks per person
Mutual free windows highlighted with green overlay
Tap/click a free window opens event creator pre-filled with date/time
Loading state while fetching FreeBusy data
Privacy indicator showing 'Only free/busy times are shared'
Responsive — works on mobile and desktop
Styled with design tokens

userStories.title: Multi-Source Event Discovery (Backend)
userStories.description: Build a backend event aggregation service that fetches events from multiple free APIs: Ticketmaster (already integrated), SeatGeek, and Eventbrite. Normalize all events into a common schema. Deduplicate by name+venue+date. Support location-based queries (by city or lat/lng).
acceptanceCriteria: SeatGeek API integration (free client_id signup)
Eventbrite API integration (free OAuth token)
Common event schema: id, source, title, description, venue, city, start_time, end_time, url, image_url
Deduplication logic for events appearing in multiple sources
GET /events/discover?city=Austin&date=2026-03-15 endpoint
Results sorted by relevance/date
Error handling per source (one failing doesn't break others)
Rate limiting awareness per API
userStories.notes: Extends existing Ticketmaster integration. SeatGeek and Eventbrite both have free, easy signups.

userStories.title: Smart Suggestions Engine
userStories.description: Build backend suggestion generation that runs on-demand or via scheduled job. Suggestion types: availability match ('You and Sarah are both free Saturday'), proximity alert ('You'll be in Austin — 3 friends live there'), event discovery ('Food festival near you both this weekend'), reconnect nudge ('Haven't seen College Friends in 6 weeks'). Store suggestions in DB, surface via API.
acceptanceCriteria: suggestions table in DB (id, user_id, type, title, body, metadata JSONB, status, created_at, expires_at)
Availability match: checks FreeBusy for mutual free windows in next 7 days
Proximity alert: detects when user's calendar has travel to a friend's city
Event discovery: cross-references free windows + nearby events from discovery service
Reconnect nudge: tracks last interaction per circle, nudges after configurable period
GET /suggestions endpoint returns user's active suggestions
PATCH /suggestions/:id/dismiss — mark as dismissed
PATCH /suggestions/:id/acted — mark as acted on
Suggestions expire automatically
userStories.notes: Depends on US-010 (FreeBusy), US-012 (event discovery). This is the 'magic' of the app.

userStories.title: Suggestions Feed Frontend
userStories.description: Build the Suggestions tab/page. Card-based feed showing smart suggestions. Each card has icon, title, body, and CTA button. Swipe to dismiss. Tap to act (opens event creator, friend profile, or event detail). Empty state with friendly messaging.
acceptanceCriteria: Suggestions page accessible via bottom tab (mobile) and sidebar (desktop)
Card-based feed layout
Each card shows type icon, title, description, and action button
Swipe to dismiss on mobile
Tap CTA navigates to appropriate action (create event, view friend, view event)
Redux suggestionSlice with async thunks
Loading, error, and empty states
Styled with design tokens (gold accents for suggestion highlights)

userStories.title: Notification System
userStories.description: Build in-app notification system. Notification bell in top bar with unread badge count. Notification types: friend requests, suggestion alerts, event invitations. Notification preferences in profile settings. Optional: web push notifications via service worker.
acceptanceCriteria: notifications table in DB
Notification bell icon in top app bar with unread badge count
Dropdown/panel showing recent notifications
Mark as read on click
Mark all as read action
Notification preferences page in profile
Real-time badge count update (polling or WebSocket)
Redux notificationSlice
Styled with design tokens
userStories.notes: Phase 3 feature. Web push is stretch goal.

userStories.title: Token Refresh & Session Persistence
userStories.description: Fix JWT token management. Currently tokens expire in 1 hour with no refresh logic. Implement token refresh flow so users don't get logged out. Handle Google OAuth token refresh using stored refresh_token. Persist session across page refreshes.
acceptanceCriteria: JWT refresh token flow implemented (issue refresh token on login)
Access token auto-refreshes before expiry
Google OAuth access_token refreshes using stored refresh_token
Session persists across page refreshes and browser restarts
Graceful handling of expired/invalid tokens (redirect to login)
No more random logouts after 1 hour
userStories.notes: Important UX fix. Currently users get silently logged out after 1 hour.

userStories.title: Privacy Controls UI
userStories.description: Build privacy settings that let users control who sees their availability. Per-friend and per-circle sharing toggles. Option to block specific time ranges from being shared. Clear UI showing exactly what is shared with whom.
acceptanceCriteria: Privacy settings section in profile page
Master toggle: share availability with friends (on/off)
Per-friend sharing toggle
Per-circle sharing toggle
Visual indicator of what friends can see (free/busy only)
Settings persisted in DB
Respected by FreeBusy API endpoint
userStories.notes: Privacy is a core value prop. Must be clear and trustworthy.

userStories.title: Location Awareness (User City + Travel Detection)
userStories.description: Add city field to user profiles. Detect travel from Google Calendar events (flights, hotels, 'Trip to X'). When a user will be in a friend's city, flag it for the suggestions engine. Simple keyword matching on event locations.
acceptanceCriteria: city column on users table (added in US-007)
Travel detection: scan Google Calendar events for travel keywords (flight, hotel, trip, travel, visiting)
Extract destination city from event title/location
Cross-reference with friends' home cities
Feed proximity data into suggestions engine
User can manually set 'I'll be in [city]' as override
userStories.notes: Depends on friends system + suggestions engine.

userStories.title: Consolidate Date Libraries
userStories.description: The frontend uses both dayjs AND date-fns. Consolidate to dayjs only (lighter weight, already used for calendar logic). Remove date-fns and date-fns-tz dependencies. Update all imports and usages.
acceptanceCriteria: All date-fns and date-fns-tz imports replaced with dayjs equivalents
dayjs timezone plugin used instead of date-fns-tz
date-fns and date-fns-tz removed from package.json
All existing date formatting and manipulation still works correctly
No runtime errors from date handling
userStories.notes: Cleanup task. Reduces bundle size and removes confusion of having two date libraries.

userStories.title: End-to-End Testing & QA
userStories.description: Comprehensive testing pass across the entire application. Unit tests for all new models and helpers. Integration tests for all API routes. Frontend component tests for key flows (auth, calendar, friends, suggestions). Fix any bugs found during testing.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend model tests: friendship, circle, suggestion models
Backend route tests: friends, circles, freebusy, suggestions, events, auth
Frontend tests: auth flow, calendar rendering, friend management, suggestion feed
All existing tests still pass
No console errors in browser
Mobile responsive testing at 320px, 375px, 768px, 1024px, 1440px
Cross-browser: Chrome, Firefox, Safari
Accessibility: keyboard navigation, screen reader basics
userStories.notes: Final phase. Run after all features are built.
